violations.text: Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2026

Deli meat was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025

Food held on the right of the hot hold unit was observed held below 135 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was placed in unit less than 2 hours ago. Foods were reheated. PIC turned up unit. Establishment to use deeper pans. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2024

Sliced vegetables were observed held above 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Establishment has food sitting on top of ice. Inspector instructed establishment of proper cold hold procedures. Food was recently placed on ice bath. Food was relocated to walk-in. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2024
The hold hold unit had 2 missing heat bulbs on the right side of the unit. Per PIC, heat lamp bulbs have been ordered. New Violation. Correct By: 06/03/2024

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 30 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/12/2023

Various food items in the deli display cooler were observed held at 43-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the deli display case. The deli display cooler was observed with an ambient temperature of 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods held 45 and above overnight were voluntarily disposed of by PIC at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/12/2023

Various items packaged in the food establishment/commissary are lacking the business address and sell by date. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 09/12/2023

The deli display case observed in poor repair (maintaining ambient temperature of 47 degrees Fahrenheit). Unit to not be used until it can be serviced/repaired/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 09/12/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the raw meat display case has an accumilation of food debris inside the unit and on the sliding doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/12/2023
